heading: Confidentiality
label: 1416 Confidentiality

children.heading: Mediation
children.label: a Mediation
children.text: All information discussed or disclosed in the course of any mediation shall be strictly confidential, and the Executive Director shall notify each person participating in the mediation of the confidentiality requirement and of the sanctions applicable to any person who violates the confidentiality requirement.

children.heading: Hearings and deliberations
children.label: b Hearings and deliberations
children.text: Except as provided in subsections (c), (d), and (e), all proceedings and deliberations of hearing officers and the Board, including any related records, shall be confidential. This subsection shall not apply to proceedings under , but shall apply to the deliberations of hearing officers and the Board under that section. The Executive Director shall notify each person participating in a proceeding or deliberation to which this subsection applies of the requirements of this subsection and of the sanctions applicable to any person who violates the requirements of this subsection.section 1341 of this title

children.heading: Release of records for judicial action
children.label: c Release of records for judicial action
children.text: The records of hearing officers and the Board may be made public if required for the purpose of judicial review under .section 1407 of this title

children.heading: Automatic referral to Congressional Ethics Committee of dispositions of claims involving Members of Congress and senior staff
children.label: d Automatic referral to Congressional Ethics Committee of dispositions of claims involving Members of Congress and senior staff
A
children.text: the Committee on Ethics of the House of Representatives, in the case of a Member or senior staff of the House; or

children.label: B
children.text: the Select Committee on Ethics of the Senate, in the case of a Senator or senior staff of the Senate.

children.heading: Access to records and information
children.label: 2 Access to records and information
children.text: If the Executive Director refers a claim to a Committee under paragraph (1), the Executive Director shall provide the Committee with access to the records of any preliminary reviews, hearings, or decisions of the hearing officers and the Board under this chapter, and any information relating to an award or settlement paid, in response to such claim.

children.heading: Review by Senate ethics committee of settlements of certain claims
children.label: 3 Review by Senate ethics committee of settlements of certain claims
A
children.text: not later than 90 days after that receipt, review the settlement agreement;

children.label: B
children.text: determine whether an investigation of the claim is warranted; and

children.label: C
children.text: if the Select Committee determines, after the investigation, that the claim that resulted in the settlement involved an actual violation described in committed personally by the Senator, then the Select Committee shall notify the Executive Director to request the reimbursement described in and include the settlement in the report required by section 1381() of this title.section 1415(d)(1)(C) of this titlesection 1415(d) of this titlel

children.heading: Protection of personally identifiable information
children.label: 4 Protection of personally identifiable information
children.text: If a Committee to which a claim is referred under paragraph (1) issues a report with respect to the claim, the Committee shall ensure that the report does not directly disclose the identity or position of the individual who filed the claim.

children.heading: Committee authority to protect identity of a claimant
children.label: 5 Committee authority to protect identity of a claimant
i
children.text: that including the information or data considered for redaction may lead to the unintentional disclosure of the identity or position of a claimant; and

children.label: ii
children.text: on the precise information or data to be redacted.

children.heading: Notation and statement
children.label: B Notation and statement
children.text: The report including any such redaction shall note each redaction and include a statement that the redaction was made solely for the purpose of avoiding such an unintentional disclosure of the identity or position of a claimant.

children.heading: Retention of reports
children.label: C Retention of reports
children.text: The Committee making a redaction in accordance with this paragraph shall retain a copy of the report, without a redaction.

children.label: A
children.text: An order or agreement to pay an award or settlement, including an agreement reached pursuant to mediation under .section 1403 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: A final decision of a hearing officer under that is no longer subject to review by the Board under .section 1405(g) of this titlesection 1406 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: A final decision of the Board under that is no longer subject to appeal to the United States Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit under .section 1406(e) of this titlesection 1407 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: A final decision in a civil action under that is no longer subject to appeal.section 1408 of this title

children.heading: Senior staff defined
children.label: 7 Senior staff defined
children.text: In this subsection, the term “senior staff” means any individual who, at the time a violation occurred, was required to file a report under title I of the Ethics in Government Act of 1978 (5 U.S.C. App. 101 et seq.).1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Final decisions
children.label: e Final decisions
children.text: A final decision entered under section 1405(g) or 1406(e) of this title shall be made public if it is in favor of the complaining covered employee, or in favor of the charging party under , or if the decision reverses a decision of a hearing officer which had been in favor of the covered employee or charging party. The Board may make public any other decision at its discretion.section 1331 of this title

children.heading: Claims
children.label: f Claims
children.text: Nothing in this section may be construed to prohibit a covered employee from disclosing the factual allegations underlying the covered employee’s claim, or to prohibit an employing office from disclosing the factual allegations underlying the employing office’s defense to the claim, in the course of any proceeding under this subchapter.
